body:
## Summary

Fixes #1642.

Makes Claude Code runs launched by `solve` quieter, deterministic, and auditable across host installs and Docker images. The configuration now has one canonical implementation and one reusable package command:

```bash
configure-claude                              # apply quiet defaults to ~/.claude/settings.json
configure-claude --verify                     # non-mutating audit; exits 1 on drift
configure-claude -s /workspace/.claude/settings.json
configure-claude --help
```

The Docker release flow now follows the latest maintainer feedback: Docker images are built only after the npm package has been published and the exact version is visible in npm. The Dockerfiles install `@link-assistant/hive-mind@${HIVE_MIND_VERSION}` and invoke the published `configure-claude` bin directly; they no longer copy repository source files or a local wrapper script into the image.

## What Changed

- Added the reusable `configure-claude` bin and shared runner for applying or verifying Hive-Mind's quiet Claude Code defaults.
- Kept built-in Claude git/PR guidance enabled with `includeGitInstructions: true`; `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS` is intentionally not set.
- Merged quiet defaults into `~/.claude/settings.json` without clobbering unrelated user `env`, `attribution`, `permissions`, or top-level settings.
- Continued forcing the required quiet env vars into every Claude subprocess launched by `solve`.
- Updated both `Dockerfile` and `coolify/Dockerfile` to install the exact published Hive-Mind package version, verify `hive --version`, run `configure-claude`, and verify the generated `/workspace/.claude/settings.json`.
- Updated release CI so Docker publish jobs wait for npm availability on each platform matrix entry and pass `HIVE_MIND_VERSION` into Docker builds.
- Replaced PR Docker image building with a static release-order contract test, because PR builds happen before the new package version exists in npm.
- Removed the old `scripts/configure-claude-quiet-defaults.mjs` Docker wrapper.
- Refreshed `docs/case-studies/issue-1642/` with the latest issue/PR data and the final release-flow analysis.

## Quiet Defaults

Environment variables forced into the Claude subprocess and baked into both Dockerfiles:

- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY=1`
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_CRON=1`
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_TERMINAL_TITLE=1`
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_CLAUDE_MDS=1`
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FAST_MODE=1`
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY=1`
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE=1`
- `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY=0`
- `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TASKS=1`
- `CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TOOL_USE_CONCURRENCY=4`
- `CLAUDE_CODE_RESUME_INTERRUPTED_TURN=1`
- `DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND=1`

Settings merged into `~/.claude/settings.json`:

- `autoMemoryEnabled: false`
- `spinnerTipsEnabled: false`
- `awaySummaryEnabled: false`
- `feedbackSurveyRate: 0`
- `includeCoAuthoredBy: false`
- `includeGitInstructions: true`
- `prefersReducedMotion: true`
- `showThinkingSummaries: false`
- `skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt: true`
- `viewMode: "verbose"`
- `attribution: { "commit": "", "pr": "" }`
- `permissions: { "defaultMode": "bypassPermissions" }`

## Reproduction

Before the original fix, the focused regression failed because `getClaudeEnv()` did not force the quiet env vars:

```text
AssertionError [ERR_ASSERTION]: getClaudeEnv should force CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY=1
undefined !== '1'
```

The new `tests/test-docker-release-order.mjs` also guards the latest Docker release-order requirement: PRs must not build Docker images before npm publish, and release Docker jobs must wait for the exact npm version and pass it into the Docker build.

## Verification

- `node tests/test-docker-release-order.mjs` — passes
- `node tests/test-claude-quiet-config.mjs` — passes
- `node tests/test-configure-claude-bin.mjs` — passes
- `node tests/useless-tools.test.mjs` — 22 passed, 0 failed
- `npm test` — passes
- `npm run lint` — clean
- `npm run format:check` — clean
- `npm run check:duplication` — clean, 10.47% duplicated lines under threshold
- `bash scripts/test-global-commands.sh` — passes, including `configure-claude --help`, apply, and `--verify`
- `bash scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh` — clean with warnings only for existing near-limit files
- `bash scripts/check-mjs-syntax.sh` — clean
- `node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs` — passes
- `node tests/docs-validation.mjs` — passes
- `git diff --check` — clean

## Notes

- No screenshots: this is runtime, package, Docker, and CI/CD configuration work, not UI work.
- Local smoke tests were run under Node `v20.20.2`, which emits the expected `EBADENGINE` warning because the package declares `node >=24.0.0`; GitHub Actions uses Node 24.

## 🤖 Quiet-config: applied this round + options report

### ✅ Applied in commit [`213e5bda`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/commit/213e5bda)

**Env vars added to `buildClaudeQuietEnv`, `getClaudeEnv`, both Dockerfiles, and the merged `~/.claude/settings.json → env`:**
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FAST_MODE=1`
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY=1` (double-check – confirmed separate from `DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND`)
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE=1`
- `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY=0` (explicit disable via the official “enable” toggle)
- `CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TOOL_USE_CONCURRENCY=4`

**Settings added to the merge helper + Dockerfile baselines (top-level keys, preserving unrelated user settings):**
- `includeCoAuthoredBy: false`
- `prefersReducedMotion: true`
- `showThinkingSummaries: false`
- `viewMode: "verbose"`
- `spinnerTipsEnabled: false` *(already present; left as-is)*
- `attribution: { "commit": "", "pr": "" }` *(new — hides Claude attribution on both git commits and PRs per https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#attribution-settings)*

All previously-required quiet keys from the issue body are still enforced (`autoMemoryEnabled`, `spinnerTipsEnabled`, `awaySummaryEnabled`, `feedbackSurveyRate` and the six original env vars).

Regression test `tests/test-claude-quiet-config.mjs` was updated to assert every new key/value, preservation of unrelated user-defined attribution/env subkeys, and Dockerfile baking. `npm test` → 41 passed / 0 failed. `npm run lint`, `npm run format:check`, `npm run check:duplication`, `scripts/check-mjs-syntax.sh`, `scripts/validate-changeset.mjs`, `tests/docs-validation.mjs`, and `scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh` (with `node_modules/` moved aside to match CI) all pass.

---

### 📋 Options report — additional env vars & settings to consider

I reviewed [`env-vars.md`](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars) and [`settings.md`](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings) end-to-end against our autonomous/Docker use case. Below is everything that **might** be relevant but that I did **not** apply — please select which ones to add and I'll wire them up in a follow-up commit.

#### Environment variables

| Variable | Suggested value | Why it might fit our autonomous/headless `solve` use | Risk of applying unconditionally |
|---|---|---|---|
| `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ADAPTIVE_THINKING` | `1` | Determinism: Opus/Sonnet 4.6 currently auto-decide thinking depth. Off = fully controlled by our `--max-thinking-budget` / `--effort-level`. | We lose automatic thinking adjustment — may increase cost or reduce quality on tasks where adaptive thinking would have scaled up. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ATTACHMENTS` | `1` | `solve` never relies on attachments; disables attachment processing path. | Would break any future feature that sends images/files to Claude. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_BACKGROUND_TASKS` | `1` | Prevents Claude from forking bash commands into background tasks that outlive the session. | May slow long-running operations Claude would normally background (docker build, CI poll). |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_BETAS` | `1` | Strips `anthropic-beta` headers for deterministic, published-only behavior. | Cuts off early access to features we may depend on later. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FILE_CHECKPOINTING` | `1` | We already use git; disables Claude's own per-file checkpoint/undo state. | Would disable `/rewind` / undo safety net. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC` | `1` | One-shot switch that implies `DISABLE_TELEMETRY`, `DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER`, `DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING`, `DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND`. | Blocks telemetry (already wanted) + error reporting — we lose Anthropic-side crash data from our prod runs. |
| `DISABLE_TELEMETRY` | `1` | No OTEL pipeline configured here, avoids spurious exports. | None that I can see — we don't configure telemetry endpoints. |
| `DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER` | `1` | Docker image should not silently self-upgrade `claude` CLI at runtime. | None for the container — we pin versions via Dockerfile. |
| `DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING` | `1` | No outbound crash reports from solve sessions. | We stop sending telemetry that helps Anthropic fix bugs that affect us. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONSTREAMING_FALLBACK` | `1` | Keeps our stream-json parsing deterministic — no silent fallback on stream hiccup. | On transient stream errors, sessions fail harder instead of degrading. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_OFFICIAL_MARKETPLACE_AUTOINSTALL` | `1` | Headless image should not pull the official plugin marketplace. | We'd need to explicitly opt-in if we ever distribute a managed plugin. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_THINKING` | *(probably no)* | Forces thinking off globally. | We actively use thinking budgets — **don't recommend**. Listed for completeness. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_VIRTUAL_SCROLL` | *(probably no)* | Fullscreen TUI only; irrelevant in `solve`'s non-TTY mode. | No effect for us. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TASKS` | `1` | Turn on task tracking in `-p` (non-interactive) mode — useful for our logs to surface internal TODO items Claude creates. | Adds output volume. |
| `CLAUDE_AUTO_BACKGROUND_TASKS` | `0` or unset | Complement to `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_BACKGROUND_TASKS`. | Same trade-offs. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_PROMPT_HISTORY` | `1` | We have our own transcript/log system; skip writing Claude's transcripts. | `/resume` and `cleanupPeriodDays` become moot. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_RESUME_INTERRUPTED_TURN` | *(depends)* | Either force-on or force-off for deterministic restart behavior after a crash mid-turn. | Depends on how we want crash recovery to behave. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_SUBPROCESS_ENV_SCRUB` | `1` | Strips credential-looking env vars from subprocesses Claude spawns (git, gh, etc.). | Could break tools we intentionally pass auth to; must verify `GH_TOKEN`/`GITHUB_TOKEN` aren't stripped. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER` / `CLAUDE_CODE_SCROLL_SPEED` / `CLAUDE_CODE_CODE_ACCESSIBILITY` / `CLAUDE_CODE_TMUX_TRUECOLOR` / `CLAUDE_CODE_SYNTAX_HIGHLIGHT` | — | UI-only, we run headless with `--output-format stream-json`. | No effect in non-TTY mode; safe to ignore. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY` | *(do NOT set)* | Opposite intent. | Don't set. |
| `BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS` / `BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS` / `BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH` | *(consider)* | We sometimes wrap long-running commands; tuning these could avoid silent truncation or timeouts. | Needs per-workflow tuning; may conflict with our existing `HIVE_MIND_*` timeouts. |
| `API_TIMEOUT_MS` | *(consider)* | Model call timeout ceiling. Default 10min. | Our `HIVE_MIND_CLAUDE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` is 60s — they operate at different layers so probably leave Claude's alone. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_GLOB_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` | *(consider)* | In huge monorepos `Glob` can be slow. Default 20s is usually fine. | None unless we hit a pathological monorepo. |
| `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSIONEND_HOOKS_TIMEOUT_MS` | *(consider)* | We don't currently ship a SessionEnd hook but may in future. | Low impact today. |

#### Settings (`~/.claude/settings.json`)

| Key | Suggested value | Why | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| `autoUpdatesChannel` | `"stable"` | Pins Claude CLI to the “stable” channel (≈1 week behind, skips regressions). Complements `DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER`. | Slightly slower feature adoption. |
| `minimumVersion` | *(per release)* | Floor to prevent CI from ever downgrading below a known-good Claude CLI. | Needs maintenance when we bump the floor. |
| `cleanupPeriodDays` | `1` | With `CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_PROMPT_HISTORY=1` this is moot; otherwise shrinks session-file retention. | Loses transcript replay window. |
| `disableAllHooks` | `true` | Our `solve` pipeline doesn't rely on user hooks — disabling neutralizes any host-side hook that could interfere. | Blocks any hook we *want* to ship later (e.g. a status-line script). |
| `disableAutoMode` | `"disable"` | We always pass explicit `--dangerously-skip-permissions`; makes sure auto-mode isn't picked up from `Shift+Tab`/user settings. | None for non-interactive runs. |
| `tui` | `"default"` | Explicit classic renderer; irrelevant in `-p` mode. | None. |
| `includeGitInstructions` | `false` | Mirror of `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS=1`. Docs: env var wins, but setting the belt makes intent visible in `/status`. | None — we already set the env var. |
| `permissions.defaultMode` | `"bypassPermissions"` | Matches `--dangerously-skip-permissions`. Makes the mode audible via `/status`. | We'd need `skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt: true` to avoid the one-time prompt. |
| `skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt` | `true` | Pairs with the above. | Makes bypass-permissions frictionless — intended for trusted CI only. |
| `outputStyle` | e.g. `"default"` or custom | Pin a specific output style so behavior is identical across hosts. | Need to pick one explicitly. |
| `statusLine` | *(skip)* | We run headless; no status line to render. | None. |
| `fastModePerSessionOptIn` | `true` | Makes sure fast mode never persists if a user enables it once. Complementary to `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FAST_MODE=1`. | None. |
| `effortLevel` | *(skip — we already pass `CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL` via env based on the model)* | — | Would conflict with dynamic per-request effort. |
| `availableModels` | e.g. `["opus","sonnet","haiku","opusplan"]` | Lock the Docker image to our known model set. | We already enforce model selection in `config.lib.mjs`. |
| `alwaysThinkingEnabled` | *(skip)* | We control thinking per-call. | Would conflict with `--max-thinking-budget`. |

#### Notes / caveats

- The three already-disabled env vars from the first round (`DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND=1`, `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY=1`) are **both** needed — docs confirm they control different things (`/feedback` command vs. session-quality survey).
- `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY` is an *enable* toggle in the env-vars docs, so `0` is the correct disable value. The mirror setting `awaySummaryEnabled: false` was already in place.
- I intentionally did **not** touch `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_*`, `ANTHROPIC_*`, `CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_*` — those change authentication/model routing/SDK behavior and shouldn't be defaulted in a quiet-config PR.

Please reply with which items above to apply and I'll land them in a follow-up commit on this branch.

CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY set to disable.

🤖 **AI Work Session Started**

Starting automated work session at 2026-04-18T16:39:57.693Z

The PR has been converted to draft mode while work is in progress.

_This comment marks the beginning of an AI work session. Please wait for the session to finish, and provide your feedback._

## 🤖 Round 2: applied maintainer feedback in [`ca20dd9d`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/commit/ca20dd9d)

Addressed every item from the latest review comment.

### ✅ Env vars (`buildClaudeQuietEnv`, `getClaudeEnv`, both Dockerfiles, `~/.claude/settings.json → env`)

| Change | Value | Why |
|---|---|---|
| 🗑️ Remove `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS` | — | Built-in git/PR workflow instructions are useful for `solve` runs. |
| ➕ `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TASKS` | `1` | Surface internal task-tracking items in non-interactive logs. |
| ➕ `CLAUDE_CODE_RESUME_INTERRUPTED_TURN` | `1` | Deterministic crash recovery — resume mid-turn after interruption. |
| ✔️ `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY=0` | kept | Already disabled via the official "enable" toggle set to `0`. |

### ✅ Settings (`~/.claude/settings.json` top-level)

| Key | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| ➕ `includeGitInstructions` | `true` | Settings-level mirror — explicitly keeps built-in git instructions on. |
| ➕ `skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt` | `true` | Skip the one-time bypass-permissions prompt for trusted CI runs. |
| ➕ `permissions.defaultMode` | `"bypassPermissions"` | Makes the bypass mode audible via `/status`; pairs with the above. |

`--dangerously-skip-permissions` is **still** passed on the CLI as belt-and-suspenders, and a new regression assertion in `tests/test-claude-quiet-config.mjs` guarantees that stays wired up.

### ✅ Merge behaviour preserves unrelated permissions subkeys

Just like `attribution` and `env`, the new `permissions` block is now a *merge* — existing keys (`additionalDirectories`, etc.) are kept. New test case asserts `permissions.additionalDirectories: ['/tmp/preserved']` survives the merge.

### ✅ Verification

- `node tests/test-claude-quiet-config.mjs` — passes (asserts every new key/value, the removal of `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS` from env+settings, `--dangerously-skip-permissions` still in `claude.lib.mjs`, Dockerfile baking of every required key)
- `node tests/useless-tools.test.mjs` — 22 passed / 0 failed
- `npm test` — 41 passed / 0 failed
- `npm run lint` — clean
- `npm run format:check` — clean
- `npm run check:duplication` — clean
- `node --check` on `src/solve.mjs`, `src/hive.mjs`, `src/claude-quiet-config.lib.mjs` — clean
- `bash scripts/check-mjs-syntax.sh` — clean
- `node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs` — passes (changeset updated)
- `node tests/docs-validation.mjs` — passes
- `bash scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh` (with `node_modules/` moved aside, matching CI job order) — clean

### 📋 Consistency notes

- The full set of required env keys is now **12** (was 11): dropped one (`DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS`), added two (`ENABLE_TASKS`, `RESUME_INTERRUPTED_TURN`), kept the rest.
- The full set of required top-level settings keys is now **10** (was 8): added `includeGitInstructions`, `skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt`.
- New nested block: `permissions` (same idempotent merge semantics as `attribution`).
- Both Dockerfiles (`Dockerfile`, `coolify/Dockerfile`) bake the new env vars via `ENV` and seed the new settings/permissions/attribution keys into the baseline `/workspace/.claude/settings.json`.
- Changeset entry at `.changeset/quiet-claude-solve.md` updated to cover the round-2 additions.

All previously-required quiet config from rounds 1–1.5 is still enforced.

This log file contains the complete execution trace of the AI solution draft process.

### 💰 Cost: **$3.496889**

Commit: `884311e1`. Addresses the latest review comment asking for a readable, maintainable, testable, deduplicated \`.mjs\` file that reuses src/ logic.

### What changed

**New file** [`scripts/configure-claude-quiet-defaults.mjs`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1642-51e94ad4ccf4/scripts/configure-claude-quiet-defaults.mjs) — ~45 lines, fully reuses:
- `ensureClaudeQuietConfig` from [`src/claude-quiet-config.lib.mjs`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1642-51e94ad4ccf4/src/claude-quiet-config.lib.mjs)
- `ensureDisallowedToolsInSettings` from [`src/useless-tools.lib.mjs`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1642-51e94ad4ccf4/src/useless-tools.lib.mjs)

No key/value lists are duplicated — the script pulls everything from the shared \`REQUIRED_CLAUDE_QUIET_ENV\` / \`REQUIRED_CLAUDE_QUIET_SETTINGS\` / \`REQUIRED_CLAUDE_QUIET_ATTRIBUTION\` / \`REQUIRED_CLAUDE_QUIET_PERMISSIONS\` / \`USELESS_CLAUDE_BUILTIN_TOOLS\` / \`USELESS_MCP_TOOL_NAME_PREFIXES\` constants used by \`solve\` and all tests.

**Dockerfile & coolify/Dockerfile** — replaced the ~25-line inline \`node -e \"...\"\` blob with:

\`\`\`dockerfile
COPY --chown=sandbox:sandbox \\
    src/claude-quiet-config.lib.mjs \\
    src/useless-tools.lib.mjs \\
    /workspace/.hive-mind-bake/src/
COPY --chown=sandbox:sandbox \\
    scripts/configure-claude-quiet-defaults.mjs \\
    /workspace/.hive-mind-bake/scripts/
RUN mkdir -p /workspace/.claude && \\
    node /workspace/.hive-mind-bake/scripts/configure-claude-quiet-defaults.mjs \\
        --settings-path /workspace/.claude/settings.json && \\
    rm -rf /workspace/.hive-mind-bake
\`\`\`

The required 12 env vars are still set via \`ENV\` at image scope (separately from the script), so the container remains quiet even if \`solve\` is bypassed.

**Tests updated**
- \`tests/test-claude-quiet-config.mjs\` — Dockerfile assertions now verify it invokes the shared script and COPIES the two src libs. A new end-to-end block spawns \`scripts/configure-claude-quiet-defaults.mjs\` against a scratch \`settings.json\` and asserts every required env / settings / attribution / permissions / disallowedTools entry is present, plus idempotency on a second run.
- \`tests/useless-tools.test.mjs\` — same Dockerfile indirection pattern for the disallowedTools assertions.

Name the command `configure-claude`, so it may be used by our users and system administrators to reset claude code configuration and verify it, so there will be no need to copy files to Dockerfiles themselves, once Hive Mind package with new command is installed. That way it will be useful and reusable for both docker and manually by our users.

## 🤖 Round 4: ship `configure-claude` as a reusable bin command

Commit: [`39657540`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/commit/39657540). Addresses the latest review comment asking for a reusable bin as part of the Hive-Mind npm package so users and sysadmins can reset/verify Claude Code configuration manually — and so Dockerfiles don't have to hand-copy files once the package is globally installed.

### What changed

**New bin** — registered in `package.json`:

```jsonc
{
  "bin": {
    "configure-claude": "./src/configure-claude.mjs",
    ...
  }
}
```

Installed via `bun install -g @link-assistant/hive-mind` (or `npm i -g ...`). Then:

```bash
configure-claude                              # apply quiet defaults to ~/.claude/settings.json
configure-claude --verify                     # non-mutating audit; exits 1 on drift
configure-claude -s /workspace/.claude/settings.json
configure-claude --help
```

**New shared runner** — [`src/configure-claude.lib.mjs`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1642-51e94ad4ccf4/src/configure-claude.lib.mjs) exposes:
- `parseConfigureClaudeArgs(argv)` — argv → `{ settingsPath, verify, help }`
- `resolveSettingsPath(path)` — fallback to `~/.claude/settings.json`
- `runConfigureClaude({ settingsPath, log })` — apply quiet settings + disallowedTools (reuses canonical maps)
- `verifyConfigureClaude({ settingsPath })` — non-mutating drift report with per-section missing keys
- `formatVerifyReport(report)` — human-readable summary

Both the bin entry point (`src/configure-claude.mjs`) and the Dockerfile wrapper (`scripts/configure-claude-quiet-defaults.mjs`) are thin delegates to this runner.

**Dockerfile / coolify/Dockerfile** continue to COPY a minimal subset (now including `src/configure-claude.lib.mjs`) into a scratch bake dir and invoke the wrapper — so PR builds work before the package is published. Once the image's `bun install -g @link-assistant/hive-mind` step picks up a released version that ships the bin, the same `configure-claude` command is also available on PATH for runtime use. Documented this in both Dockerfiles.

**Tests** — [`tests/test-configure-claude-bin.mjs`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1642-51e94ad4ccf4/tests/test-configure-claude-bin.mjs) (new, wired into `npm test`) covers:
- package.json `bin` + `build:pre` registration contract
- `parseConfigureClaudeArgs` for every flag form
- `resolveSettingsPath` default vs. explicit
- Help text content (command name, `--verify`, issue reference)
- `verifyConfigureClaude` drift on missing file
- End-to-end `configure-claude` apply over a fresh tmp settings.json (every env / settings / attribution / permissions / disallowedTools entry present)
- `--verify` exits 0 on correct config, exits 1 after tampering
- Second apply is idempotent
- `--help` prints usage

The existing [`tests/test-claude-quiet-config.mjs`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1642-51e94ad4ccf4/tests/test-claude-quiet-config.mjs) was extended to also require that both Dockerfiles COPY the new shared runner.

**Changeset** — [`quiet-claude-solve.md`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1642-51e94ad4ccf4/.changeset/quiet-claude-solve.md) was amended to describe the new bin command so the next release includes it.

### Verification

- `node tests/test-claude-quiet-config.mjs` — passes
- `node tests/test-configure-claude-bin.mjs` — passes (new)
- `npm test` — 41 passed, 0 failed
- `npm run lint` / `npm run format:check` / `npm run check:duplication` — clean
- `bash scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh` — clean (node_modules moved aside, matching CI)
- `node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs` — passes

### Why this matches the ask

> "set bin reusable command as part of Hive Mind package … name the command `configure-claude`, so it may be used by our users and system administrators to reset claude code configuration and verify it"

- `bin` entry ✅
- name `configure-claude` ✅
- reset (default apply) + verify (`--verify`) ✅
- reusable from users, sysadmins, Dockerfiles, tests — one shared runner ✅
- ships via `@link-assistant/hive-mind` on npm ✅

## 🤖 Round 6: restore Docker PR check + keep release-order strict

Commit: [`c5207943`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/commit/c5207943). Addresses the latest review comment — the previous commit `dd75288b` over-rotated by replacing the PR Docker build with only a static contract test, which is not what was asked.

### What changed

**`.github/workflows/release.yml` → `docker-pr-check`**
- Kept the static release-order contract test (`node tests/test-docker-release-order.mjs`) — it's still a useful pre-build guard against regressions of the release-order rule.
- Restored the real Docker build steps: disk free-up, sandbox base version echo, `docker build --progress=plain -t konard/hive-mind:test .`, and `docker run … bash /verify-docker-image.sh`.

**`Dockerfile` and `coolify/Dockerfile`**
- The `configure-claude` step now branches on `HIVE_MIND_VERSION`:
  - **Release builds** (`HIVE_MIND_VERSION=<exact>`) — `configure-claude` must exist and must succeed. Build fails otherwise.
  - **PR builds** (`HIVE_MIND_VERSION=latest`) — if `configure-claude` isn't in the currently published `@link-assistant/hive-mind@latest` yet (which is the case for this PR, since the bin is introduced here), the build logs and skips the bake-time baseline. `solve` re-applies the quiet baseline at runtime.

**`scripts/verify-docker-image.sh`**
- Same tolerance: warn instead of fail when `configure-claude` is absent. When present, the verify script still runs `configure-claude --help` and `configure-claude --verify` end-to-end.

**`tests/test-docker-release-order.mjs`**
- Now asserts the opposite of before: the PR check **must** run `docker build --progress=plain` and `bash /verify-docker-image.sh`. Also asserts both Dockerfiles branch on `HIVE_MIND_VERSION` and tolerate a missing `configure-claude` bin on `latest`.
- Still asserts the release-order contract: every Docker matrix entry waits on npm, both `docker-publish` and the instant variant pass `HIVE_MIND_VERSION` from the release step, and both depend on their respective release jobs.

### Release-order contract preserved
1. `main` push → `release` job publishes `@link-assistant/hive-mind@X.Y.Z` to npm.
2. `docker-publish` waits for npm to serve `X.Y.Z` on every platform matrix entry.
3. Docker build receives `HIVE_MIND_VERSION=X.Y.Z`, installs that exact version, and runs `configure-claude` strictly.

### PR flow preserved
1. PR opens → `docker-pr-check` runs contract test, then does a real `docker build .` with `HIVE_MIND_VERSION=latest` (default from `ARG`).
2. Inside the build, the published `@link-assistant/hive-mind@latest` is installed. If the currently published latest doesn't yet have `configure-claude`, the step logs and skips — this PR itself is what will publish the bin.
3. `scripts/verify-docker-image.sh` runs inside the container and verifies every tool (base + AI) and tolerantly checks `configure-claude`.

### Local verification

- `node tests/test-docker-release-order.mjs` — passes
- `node tests/test-claude-quiet-config.mjs` — passes
- `node tests/test-configure-claude-bin.mjs` — passes
- `node tests/useless-tools.test.mjs` — 22 passed, 0 failed
- `npm test` — 41 passed, 0 failed
- `npm run lint` — clean
- `npm run format:check` — clean
- `npm run check:duplication` — clean (10.47% ≤ threshold)
- `bash scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh` — clean
- `bash scripts/check-mjs-syntax.sh` — clean
- `node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs` — passes
- `node tests/docs-validation.mjs` — passes

commits.messageHeadline: fix: enforce quiet claude solve configuration

commits.messageBody:
Adds five new env vars (CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FAST_MODE,
CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY, CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE,
CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY=0, CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TOOL_USE_CONCURRENCY=4)
and four new settings (includeCoAuthoredBy=false, prefersReducedMotion=true,
showThinkingSummaries=false, viewMode="verbose") plus attribution.commit/pr
overrides to hide Claude attribution on commits/PRs. Updates solve runtime
merge helper, both Dockerfiles, the case-study doc, and the regression test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: feat(quiet-config): expand Claude Code quiet defaults per #1642 feedback

commits.messageBody:
Address konard's review comment asking to:

1. Re-enable Claude's built-in git/PR instructions — they are useful for
   solve runs. Drop `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS=1` from both
   `buildClaudeQuietEnv`, `getClaudeEnv` overlay, both Dockerfile `ENV`
   blocks, and the baseline `~/.claude/settings.json env` seed. Mirror
   the intent at the settings level with `includeGitInstructions: true`.

2. Enable task tracking in non-interactive mode with
   `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TASKS=1` so internal TODO items surface in logs.

3. Enable turn resume with `CLAUDE_CODE_RESUME_INTERRUPTED_TURN=1` for
   deterministic crash recovery.

4. Make the bypass-permissions mode visible in `/status` and skip the
   one-time prompt via `permissions.defaultMode: "bypassPermissions"`
   and `skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt: true`. The existing
   `--dangerously-skip-permissions` CLI flag is kept as belt-and-
   suspenders.

5. Preserve unrelated user-defined `permissions.*` subkeys (e.g.
   `additionalDirectories`) by merging, same pattern as `attribution`
   and `env`.

Regression test updated to assert:
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS` is neither forced via env nor
  written into the merged settings env block.
- `claude.lib.mjs` still passes `--dangerously-skip-permissions`.
- `permissions.additionalDirectories` is preserved across merges.
- Every new key/value is baked into both Dockerfiles.

`npm test` → 41 passed / 0 failed. `npm run lint`, `npm run format:check`,
`npm run check:duplication`, `scripts/check-mjs-syntax.sh`,
`scripts/validate-changeset.mjs`, `tests/docs-validation.mjs`, and
`scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh` (with node_modules/ moved aside to
match CI) all pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: feat(quiet-config): apply PR #1643 round-2 maintainer feedback

commits.messageBody:
…ts/*.mjs

Replaces the long `node -e "..."` blocks in both Dockerfiles with a dedicated
scripts/configure-claude-quiet-defaults.mjs that reuses the canonical
required-env / settings / attribution / permissions maps and the idempotent
merge helpers from src/claude-quiet-config.lib.mjs and
src/useless-tools.lib.mjs, so the Dockerfile, solve command, and tests stay
in lock-step.

Addresses the latest PR #1643 review comment asking for a readable, testable,
deduplicated mjs file that reuses src/ logic instead of reinventing it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: feat(quiet-config): extract Dockerfile inline node -e to shared scrip…

commits.messageBody:
Addresses the PR #1643 round-4 maintainer feedback asking for a reusable
`configure-claude` bin command on the `@link-assistant/hive-mind` npm
package so that users and system administrators can reset or audit the
quiet Claude Code configuration manually — and so the Docker image does
not need to copy source files once the package is globally installed.

- Adds `src/configure-claude.mjs` as the bin entry point and
  `src/configure-claude.lib.mjs` as the shared runner (apply + --verify
  check-only mode), both reusing the canonical env/settings/attribution/
  permissions maps and idempotent merge helpers from
  `src/claude-quiet-config.lib.mjs` and `src/useless-tools.lib.mjs`.
- Wires `configure-claude` into the `bin` map of package.json and marks
  the entry executable via `build:pre`.
- Collapses `scripts/configure-claude-quiet-defaults.mjs` to a thin
  wrapper that delegates to the shared runner so the Dockerfile, the
  published CLI, and the dev-time script all exercise one code path.
- Updates both Dockerfiles to also COPY `src/configure-claude.lib.mjs`
  (the shared runner the wrapper imports) and documents that the bin
  becomes available on PATH after `bun install -g @link-assistant/hive-mind`.
- Adds `tests/test-configure-claude-bin.mjs` covering arg parsing, the
  package.json bin contract, apply, --verify success and drift cases,
  idempotency, and --help. Extends the existing quiet-config test to
  require both Dockerfiles COPY the new shared runner.
- Updates the existing changeset describing the new bin command.
commits.messageHeadline: feat(configure-claude): ship reusable bin for quiet Claude Code defaults
fix(docker): build from published hive-mind package

commits.messageBody:
PR #1643 round-6 feedback: the previous commit replaced the PR Docker build
with a static contract test. The maintainer wants Docker to be actually built
and verified on every PR — but the build must tolerate a `configure-claude`
bin that hasn't been published to npm yet (since PRs install
@link-assistant/hive-mind@latest, which predates this very PR).

- Dockerfile / coolify/Dockerfile: branch on HIVE_MIND_VERSION. Release
  builds (pinned version) strictly require configure-claude; PR builds
  (latest) skip-with-log if the bin isn't present — solve re-applies the
  quiet baseline at runtime.
- scripts/verify-docker-image.sh: same tolerance — warn instead of fail
  when configure-claude is absent.
- .github/workflows/release.yml docker-pr-check: restored the real build
  and in-container verification steps; kept the static release-order
  contract test before the build so the CI/CD contract stays explicit.
- tests/test-docker-release-order.mjs: assert PR check now builds and
  verifies the image; assert Dockerfiles branch on HIVE_MIND_VERSION and
  gracefully handle a missing configure-claude bin.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.messageHeadline: fix(ci): restore Docker PR build, keep release exact-version enforcement

title: Disable noisy Claude Code features in solve and Docker
